Telanthera is a flowering plant known for its vibrant red flowers. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Telanthera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Telanthera is often made into a tea or a tincture, and is used as a treatment for various ailments, including respiratory infections and fever.
